desc: Bodrum is a whitewashed Aegean peninsula town known for beach clubs, castle views, and easy boat days around sheltered bays.
hls: Monthly climate fields are imported from the shared climate CSV and verified against source rows.
Shoulder seasons typically provide the best comfort-to-crowd trade-off for most visitors.
Peak-season periods raise crowding and accommodation pressure, so booking ahead helps.

todo.name: Historic core walk
todo.desc: Start with an orientation walk through the most characterful central districts.

todo.name: Top local food stop
todo.desc: Plan one meal around regional specialties in a well-reviewed local venue.

todo.name: Half-day nature or coast outing
todo.desc: Keep one flexible half-day for a nearby scenic area or waterfront route.

wifi.note: City-center accommodations and cafés are usually dependable; confirm speed if you need to work remotely.

prac.fltNote: Bodrum has strong seasonal nonstop UK service; Dalaman is a practical fallback when BJV schedules are thinner.
prac.currency: Check current local currency usage and card acceptance before departure.
prac.lang: Local language is dominant; English support is strongest in tourism-facing venues.
prac.tz: Confirm local UTC offset and daylight-saving rules for your exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Entry requirements depend on nationality and can change; verify official guidance before booking.
alerts: July-August marina and beach-club demand pushes rates up quickly; lock in key bookings early.
Meltemi winds can make some boat routes choppy; confirm departure conditions the same day.
Late-night peninsula transfers are limited outside major hubs; prebook return transport.

sweet: May-June and September-October usually balance warm sea weather with lighter crowds than peak summer.
